title: [lichen_l2.c:1300-1306] re-init path does not clear peer_table before lichen_link_init
description: In lichen_l2_iface_init, when link_ctx_initialized is true (re-initialization path), the code at lines 1301-1306 acquires mutexes and calls lichen_link_cleanup(), but does NOT clear peer_table. The peer_table is only cleared at line 1319 with memset AFTER the mutexes are released. This means during re-init, stale peer entries could be accessed between lines 1304 (cleanup) and 1319 (memset). Also, line 1319 uses memset instead of secure_zero, unlike the disable path at lines 1071-1074 which uses secure_zero for pubkeys. Security inconsistency.
close_reason: Fixed: peer_table now cleared with secure_zero inside mutex-protected re-init path
